title: Chapter 7: The Gilded Cage
content: The afternoon sun slices through the floor-to-ceiling windows of the Atlas Tower, gilding the dust motes in the air, but the office remains ice-cold. Alistair Thorne doesn't turn around immediately. He stands by the window, watching the city below like a king surveying his domain.||'My secretary was quite impressed by your... argument for this meeting, Detective,' he says, his voice a smooth, cultured baritone. 'Mentioning Sterling's documents is a dangerous way to get my attention. I hope you didn't come here under the illusion that we are equals.'

choices.text: Professional Curiosity: 'Sterling left me more than just numbers, Thorne. He left me questions.'
Direct Bluff: 'I'm here to find out what the silence of a man in my position is worth to you.'
